name: get_comps
description:
Find comparable recent sales for a property and summarize a suggested value range.

Give a subject either by reference (listing_key, or mls_number [+ originating_system]) or inline: an area (city / postal_code / county — required unless you pass latitude+longitude) plus whatever you know (property_type, living_area, bedrooms, bathrooms_full, year_built).

Comps are recent CLOSED sales of the same product type in the subject's area (default: last ~180 days). When the subject has coordinates the pool is limited to a radius and distance feeds the score; most feeds (including MRED) omit coordinates, so comps then fall back to the same city/area and distance is omitted. Each comp carries a 0..1 similarity score (a heuristic over size, beds/baths, age, and distance when available) with short adjust_notes explaining it. The result includes median_close_price, median_ppsf, and a suggested_low/suggested_high range (interquartile $/sqft × the subject's size, or interquartile close price when size is unknown).

Use it for a quick CMA (agents), resale/ARV sanity checks (investors), or to justify an offer (buyers). It is decision support, not an appraisal: with few nearby sales the range widens and confidence drops.

city.description: inline subject area: city (required unless coordinates or another area field is given)

closed_within_days.description: only consider sales closed within this many days (default ~180)

county.description: inline subject area: county or parish

latitude.description: inline subject latitude; enables radius + distance scoring

limit.description: maximum comps to return (default 5, capped at 25)

living_area.description: inline subject living area in square feet (drives the suggested range)

mls_number.description: subject by MLS number; add originating_system if it exists in more than one feed

originating_system.description: originating system slug to disambiguate mls_number

property_type.description: inline subject product type, e.g. Residential

radius_miles.description: search radius in miles when the subject has coordinates (default ~1)

street_name.description: street name including directional prefix/suffix as delivered

unit_number.description: unit or apartment number, if any

adjust_notes.description: human-readable rationale for the score (size/bed/age/distance deltas)

close_price.description: sale price in whole US dollars; present only for closed listings

distance_miles.description: miles from the subject; omitted when coordinates are unavailable

latitude.description: latitude; often absent — many MLSs (incl. MRED) omit coordinates

list_price.description: list price in whole US dollars

listing_key.description: MLS Grid cross-MLS unique key, e.g. MRD12345678; use it with get_listing

mls_number.description: human-facing MLS number (ListingId); unique only within an originating system

modification_ts.description: when MLS Grid last modified this record (RFC3339 UTC)

property_sub_type.description: RESO PropertySubType, e.g. Single Family Residence, Condominium

property_type.description: RESO PropertyType, e.g. Residential, Residential Income

standard_status.description: RESO StandardStatus, e.g. Active, Closed, Pending

data_as_of.description: how current the underlying data is (RFC3339 UTC)

median_close_price.description: median comp sale price in whole US dollars

median_ppsf.description: median comp price per square foot in whole US dollars

suggested_high.description: high end of the suggested value range in whole US dollars

suggested_low.description: low end of the suggested value range in whole US dollars

name: get_data_freshness
description:
Report how current the underlying MLS data is before you rely on the other tools.

Returns, per synced resource, the replication cursor (newest listing update seen, whether the initial backfill finished, and when the last full reconcile ran), plus the listing corpus broken down by status, media download coverage, the schema-contract version the data conforms to, and when this snapshot was taken.

Use it to decide whether the dataset is fresh enough to trust for an answer, to explain staleness to the user, or simply to confirm the sync pipeline is alive. Takes no arguments.

contract_version.description: the mlsgrid-sync schema-contract version the data conforms to

cursors.description: replication state per synced resource and MLS feed

backfill_complete.description: whether the initial full import finished

last_reconcile.description: when the last full-feed reconcile completed (RFC3339 UTC); empty if never

resource.description: RESO resource name, e.g. Property or OpenHouse

stored_rows.description: rows of this resource held locally

watermark.description: newest ModificationTimestamp synced (RFC3339 UTC); empty before first sync

data_as_of.description: newest listing modification timestamp (RFC3339 UTC) — how current the data is; empty if unknown

generated_at.description: when this freshness snapshot was produced (RFC3339 UTC)

listing_status_counts.description: listing counts by standard status, most populous first

total_listings.description: total number of property listings stored

name: get_listing
description:
Fetch full detail for one listing by its ListingKey or MLS number.

Prefer listing_key (the cross-MLS unique key, e.g. MRD12345678) — it is unambiguous and is what search_listings returns. mls_number is the human-facing MLS number and is unique only within an originating system, so if the same number exists in more than one feed you must also pass originating_system; otherwise the lookup reports the reference as ambiguous.

Set include_raw to also return the source's scope-filtered extra fields (investment/expense fields, feature arrays, MLS-local keys) as a raw JSON object — useful for details the curated columns do not cover.

Returns list/original/close price (whole US dollars), beds/baths/size/year, address, agent and office, taxes and association fee, photo count and virtual tour, and a data_as_of timestamp. Money is whole dollars; coordinates are often absent.

include_raw.description: also return the scope-filtered raw JSONB extras as a raw object

listing_key.description: MLS Grid cross-MLS unique key (preferred, unambiguous)

mls_number.description: human-facing MLS number; unique only within an originating system

originating_system.description: originating system slug (e.g. mred) to disambiguate mls_number

street_name.description: street name including directional prefix/suffix as delivered

unit_number.description: unit or apartment number, if any

association_fee.description: association/HOA fee in whole US dollars

close_price.description: sale price in whole US dollars; present only for closed listings

data_as_of.description: how current this record is (its modification timestamp, RFC3339 UTC)

latitude.description: latitude; often absent — many MLSs (incl. MRED) omit coordinates

list_price.description: list price in whole US dollars

listing_key.description: MLS Grid cross-MLS unique key, e.g. MRD12345678; use it with get_listing

mls_number.description: human-facing MLS number (ListingId); unique only within an originating system

modification_ts.description: when MLS Grid last modified this record (RFC3339 UTC)

original_list_price.description: original list price in whole US dollars

photos_count.description: number of photos on the listing

property_sub_type.description: RESO PropertySubType, e.g. Single Family Residence, Condominium

property_type.description: RESO PropertyType, e.g. Residential, Residential Income

raw.description: scope-filtered raw extras; present only when include_raw is set

standard_status.description: RESO StandardStatus, e.g. Active, Closed, Pending

tax_annual_amount.description: annual property tax in whole US dollars

tax_year.description: tax year the amount applies to

virtual_tour_url.description: unbranded virtual tour URL, if any

name: get_open_houses
description:
List scheduled open houses for an area and date range.

Optionally scope by area (city / postal_code / county / state) and bound with from / to dates (YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339). Defaults to the next 7 days from today. Results are ordered by date, then start time; limit defaults to 25 (capped at 100). Each entry carries the listing_key (use it with get_listing for full detail), the address, the date and start/end times, and any public remarks.

Use it to plan a showing route (agents/buyers) or to see which comparable listings are actively being marketed this week. The schedule is only as current as the last sync — data_as_of tells you when that was, and an open house synced days ago may since have been cancelled.

from.description: start of the date window, YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339 (default today)

limit.description: maximum open houses to return (default 25, capped at 100)

to.description: end of the date window, inclusive, YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339 (default from + 7 days)

data_as_of.description: how current the open-house schedule is (RFC3339 UTC)

address.description: listing address (from the joined property record)

street_name.description: street name including directional prefix/suffix as delivered

unit_number.description: unit or apartment number, if any

listing_key.description: MLS Grid key of the listing; use with get_listing

mls_number.description: human-facing MLS number of the listing

name: market_stats
description:
Aggregate an area's market over a trailing window: pricing, pace, and supply.

Requires an area (city / postal_code / county / state — it will not aggregate the whole feed). Optionally narrow by property_types and set period_days (default 90). Money is in whole US dollars.

Returns two groups of numbers. Current for-sale snapshot: median_list_price, active_inventory, and months_of_supply (active inventory ÷ the monthly closed-sale rate — under ~6 favors sellers, over ~6 favors buyers). Sales closed in the period: median/avg close price, median_ppsf, median days on market (both median_days_on_market and median_cumulative_days_on_market, which counts relists and exposes DOM-reset gaming), and sale-to-list reported two ways — sale_to_list_ratio (vs the final list price) and sale_to_original_ratio (vs the original ask, showing total markdown). Set compare_to_prior to also get the immediately preceding window as prior (closed-sale metrics only; inventory is a current snapshot and is not reconstructed for the past).

Personas: agents gauge market temperature for a pricing conversation; investors read months_of_supply and sale-to-list for timing and negotiation room; buyers/sellers learn whether it is a buyer's or seller's market. Honest limit: these are medians over whatever closed in the window, so a thin area or short period yields small samples — widen period_days when counts are low.

city.description: area: city (required unless another area field is set); case-insensitive exact match

compare_to_prior.description: also aggregate the immediately preceding window and return it as prior

period_days.description: trailing window in days for closed-sale metrics (default 90)

property_types.description: limit to these PropertyType or PropertySubType values; empty means all types

active_inventory.description: count of active listings in the area/type right now

avg_close_price.description: average close price of sales in the period, whole US dollars

closed_in_period.description: number of sales that closed in the period

data_as_of.description: how current the underlying data is (RFC3339 UTC)

median_close_price.description: median close price of sales in the period, whole US dollars

median_cumulative_days_on_market.description: median cumulative days on market (across relists); a gap vs median_days_on_market suggests DOM resets

median_days_on_market.description: median days on market of closed sales

median_list_price.description: median list price of active inventory, whole US dollars

median_ppsf.description: median close price per square foot, whole US dollars

months_of_supply.description: active inventory ÷ monthly closed-sale rate; under ~6 favors sellers

period_days.description: trailing window aggregated over, in days

prior.description: the immediately preceding window when compare_to_prior is set

avg_close_price.description: average close price in whole US dollars

closed_in_period.description: number of sales closed in this prior window

median_close_price.description: median close price in whole US dollars

median_cumulative_days_on_market.description: median cumulative days on market (across relists)

median_days_on_market.description: median days on market of closed sales

median_ppsf.description: median close price per square foot in whole US dollars

period_days.description: length of this prior window in days

sale_to_list_ratio.description: median close ÷ final list price (negotiation off the last ask)

sale_to_original_ratio.description: median close ÷ original list price (total markdown from first ask)

name: price_history
description:
Return a listing's observed price and status change timeline.

Identify the listing the same way as get_listing: by listing_key (preferred) or by mls_number (add originating_system if the number exists in more than one feed). Returns each captured event (new listing, price change, status change, back on market, delisted) with its old/new value and when it was observed, plus total_reduction_pct (net list-price change as a percent of the first observed price; positive means the price came down) and days_since_last_change.

Important: history is best-effort from first sync forward — there is no event for a change that happened before this database began tracking the listing, so an empty or short timeline does not mean the listing never changed. Use it to spot motivated sellers (repeated cuts, long time since last change) and to explain a price's trajectory.

listing_key.description: MLS Grid cross-MLS unique key (preferred, unambiguous)

mls_number.description: human-facing MLS number; unique only within an originating system

originating_system.description: originating system slug (e.g. mred) to disambiguate mls_number

data_as_of.description: how current this listing's record is (RFC3339 UTC)

days_since_last_change.description: whole days since the most recent observed change; 0 if none

events.description: observed changes, oldest first; may be empty

event_type.description: one of new_listing, price_change, status_change, back_on_market, delisted

observed_at.description: when the sync observed the change (RFC3339 UTC)

listing_key.description: the listing this history belongs to

total_reduction_pct.description: net list-price change as a percent of the first observed price; positive means a reduction

name: query_sql
description:
Run a single read-only SQL SELECT against the MLS database, for questions the curated tools can't express.

PREFER THE CURATED TOOLS. search_listings, get_listing, get_comps, market_stats, price_history, and get_open_houses return documented, well-shaped results and should answer almost everything. Reach for query_sql only for a one-off aggregation, join, or filter the other tools don't cover.

Rules enforced by the server: exactly one statement; it must be a SELECT (or WITH … SELECT); writes, DDL, multiple statements, and server-side file/IO functions are rejected. The query runs in a read-only transaction under a short statement timeout, and results are row-capped — check "truncated" and raise max_rows or add your own LIMIT/aggregation if it is set.

Table and column names follow the mlsgrid-sync schema contract, and unqualified names resolve to the data schema. To discover names, query information_schema (e.g. SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema = current_schema()). This tool is only present when the operator has explicitly enabled it against a least-privilege read-only role.

max_rows.description: maximum rows to return; the server applies a default and enforces a hard ceiling

query.description: a single read-only SQL SELECT (or WITH … SELECT) statement

data_as_of.description: how current the underlying data is (RFC3339 UTC)

rows.description: result rows; each row is an array of values aligned to columns

truncated.description: true when more rows matched than were returned — raise max_rows or narrow the query

name: search_listings
description:
Search the MLS listing corpus by area, status, type, price, size, and free text.

Use this to find candidate properties before drilling into one with get_listing. All filters are optional and AND together; omit a filter to leave that dimension unconstrained. Money is in whole US dollars. Results are ordered newest-updated first.

Notes for accurate use:
- Area: set at most one of city / postal_code / county (state narrows further). Matching is case-insensitive and exact — pass "Chicago", not "chicago area".
- statuses / property_types accept several values (OR within a field); a property_types value matches either PropertyType or PropertySubType.
- keywords is a best-effort substring match over remarks and address (no full-text index), so it is a filter of last resort, not a semantic search.
- Coordinates (latitude/longitude) are frequently absent — many MLSs, including MRED, omit them.
- Paginate with the returned next_cursor (pass it back as cursor); an empty next_cursor means the last page. No grand total is returned — page through instead.

Personas: buyers/investors filter by area+price+beds; agents pull recent activity in a market; analysts sweep a status/type across a county.

city.description: city to search within (case-insensitive exact match)

county.description: county or parish to search within

cursor.description: opaque pagination cursor from a prior result's next_cursor

keywords.description: best-effort substring match over remarks and address

limit.description: maximum results to return (default 25, capped at 100)

max_living_area.description: maximum living area in square feet

max_price.description: maximum list price in whole US dollars

min_living_area.description: minimum living area in square feet

min_price.description: minimum list price in whole US dollars

property_types.description: PropertyType or PropertySubType values to include; empty means any

state.description: two-letter state or province to narrow the area

statuses.description: RESO StandardStatus values to include, e.g. ["Active","Pending"]; empty means any

count.description: number of listings on this page

data_as_of.description: how current the underlying data is (newest record timestamp, RFC3339 UTC)

street_name.description: street name including directional prefix/suffix as delivered

unit_number.description: unit or apartment number, if any

close_price.description: sale price in whole US dollars; present only for closed listings

latitude.description: latitude; often absent — many MLSs (incl. MRED) omit coordinates

list_price.description: list price in whole US dollars

listing_key.description: MLS Grid cross-MLS unique key, e.g. MRD12345678; use it with get_listing

mls_number.description: human-facing MLS number (ListingId); unique only within an originating system

modification_ts.description: when MLS Grid last modified this record (RFC3339 UTC)

property_sub_type.description: RESO PropertySubType, e.g. Single Family Residence, Condominium

property_type.description: RESO PropertyType, e.g. Residential, Residential Income

standard_status.description: RESO StandardStatus, e.g. Active, Closed, Pending

next_cursor.description: pass back as cursor to fetch the next page; empty on the last page
